metaDescription: Frequently asked questions about SIT, the student association for HBO-ICT at the HvA. What it costs, what you get, and how to join.
ogDescription: Everything you want to know about joining SIT.

structuredData.a1: SIT is the student association for HBO-ICT students at the Amsterdam University of Applied Sciences. We organize events, workshops, hackathons, game nights and more. With {members} members and {commissies} committees there is always something to do.
structuredData.a2: Membership is a one time fee of {price} per year. No hidden costs, no monthly subscriptions. You can pay with iDEAL or credit card.
structuredData.q3: What do I get as a member?
structuredData.a3: Access to all SIT events (most are free), free dev tools through the GitHub Student Pack, a personal member card, committee participation, project opportunities, and a community of fellow ICT students.
structuredData.q4: Do I have to be a student to join?
structuredData.a4: No. SIT is open to everyone: students, teachers, alumni and externals.
structuredData.a5: Most events are free for members. Some events (like parties or external venues) have a small fee. The price is always listed with the event.
structuredData.a6: Committees are teams of members who take on specific tasks: organizing events, running the socials, making educational content, organizing games, and more.
structuredData.q7: How does the XP / leaderboard system work?
structuredData.a7: You earn XP by attending events, collecting badges and staying active. This levels you up and gets you on the leaderboard. It is completely optional.
structuredData.a8: Yes. When you register you can pick 'Look around first'. Your account is then created without payment.
structuredData.q9: How do I reach the board?
structuredData.a9: Send a mail to {email}, DM us on Instagram ({instagram}) or TikTok ({tiktok}), or join our WhatsApp group or Discord server.
